---
applyTo: "**/*.{md,mdx,json,yml,yaml}"
description: "Use when creating or reviewing level design documents from ideas, especially flow, mechanics, narrative, and map sections."
---

# Level Design Document Instructions

Use these rules whenever you produce a level design document for this project.

## Required Output Shape
Include all sections below in this exact order:
1. Overview and design intent
2. Player experience goals
3. Flow and pacing
4. Mechanics and system interactions
5. Narrative and environmental storytelling
6. Map layout and wayfinding
7. Encounter and challenge curve
8. Validation plan and open questions

## Writing Rules
- Start each section with 2 to 4 outcome-focused bullets.
- Prefer concrete, testable statements over adjectives.
- Mark assumptions and unknown values as `[PLACEHOLDER]`.
- Keep tuning variables explicit (timings, counts, rewards, risks).
- Tie narrative beats to map locations, not abstract lore only.

## Map and Flow Minimums
- Provide a critical path and at least one optional path.
- Identify checkpoints, choke points, and recovery spaces.
- Note intended tension curve across beginning, middle, and end.
- Explain how players read navigation cues at each branch.

## Acceptance Checklist
- Reader can summarize the level in under 60 seconds.
- Another designer can recreate the level structure from the document.
- Mechanics, narrative, and map sections reference each other consistently.
- At least one playtest or review method is defined.
